lesson.menu_title: Pronunciation Lab II — Stress, Intonation & Confidence
lesson.menu_desc: Word stress on long words, sentence stress (content vs function words), rising vs falling intonation, and emphatic stress to sound clear and confident — con-FE-rence, RE-com-men-DA-tion, and "I said the FIRST option, not the second."
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 — Pronunciation Lab II: Stress, Intonation & Confidence
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 20 -- Pronunciation Lab II: Stress & Intonation

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, one short tip about stress and intonation, the music of English. Two things make you sound clear and confident. First, word stress. Long words have one strong syllable. We say con-FE-rence, re-com-men-DA-tion, and pri-O-ri-tize. The strong syllable is louder and longer. If you stress the wrong part, people get confused. Second, sentence stress. In a sentence, we stress the content words, the ones with meaning, like nouns and verbs, and we make the small function words, like the, to, and of, very short and weak. Listen. The PROJECT is on SCHEDule. We do not stress is or on. Now intonation, the up and down of your voice. Questions often go up at the end. Are you READY? Statements go down. I am READY. And for confidence, use emphatic stress. To correct someone, push one word hard. I said the FIRST option, not the second. That one strong word makes you sound sure, not nervous. So, do not speak in a flat monotone. Vary your pace, stress the important words, and let your voice rise and fall. That is confident English. Try it today. Talk soon.

Hi Daniela, it is Greg. Quick voicemail before your big presentation tomorrow. I listened to your practice run, and honestly, your English is really good. Just one small thing about delivery. You sometimes speak in a flat monotone, all on one level, and it hides how much you know. So tomorrow, remember three things. One, slow your pace a little, do not rush. Two, stress the key words. When you say the most important point, push it. Our REVENUE grew by twenty percent. Make grew and twenty strong. Three, use your intonation. Let your voice go up for questions and down for strong statements, it sounds confident. And if someone challenges a number, do not get nervous, just use emphatic stress to highlight the fact. I said twenty percent, not twelve. One strong word, and you sound completely in control. You have got this, Daniela. The content is there, now just let your voice carry it. Project your voice, speak with confidence, and you will be great. Good luck tomorrow. Talk soon.
